text.title: Qdrant — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules: distance + HNSW at collection creation, payload indexes for filters, quantization tradeoffs, snapshot before schema change, named vectors for multi-modal.

rule.statement: Distance metric + HNSW m + ef_construct MUST be set at collection creation via `VectorParams` / `HnswConfigDiff`. Changing m or distance requires recreating the collection.
rule.rationale: m is baked into the graph topology; m change requires a full re-index.

rule.statement: For every payload field referenced in a `Filter` clause, a payload index MUST be created. Filter-only retrieval on unindexed fields scans the entire collection.
rule.rationale: Unindexed payload filter scans degrade by O(N); a payload index brings it to sub-millisecond at high cardinality.

rule.statement: Scalar quantization (Int8) halves RAM with <1% recall loss on dense models; binary quantization gives ~32x but ONLY on high-dim (≥1024) models, with rescoring enabled.
rule.rationale: Binary on low-dim collapses recall; scalar is the safer default.

rule.statement: Before any schema change (collection recreate, quantization toggle, named-vector add) a point-level snapshot MUST be taken via `snapshot_collection` and verified restorable.
rule.rationale: Schema changes are destructive; without a verified snapshot, rollback is impossible.

rule.statement: Multi-modal data (text + image) MUST use named vectors on a single point, NOT separate points per modality.
rule.rationale: Separate points break joint ranking; named vectors keep one logical document searchable across modalities.
